content.cn_text: 你将听到一段关于农村发展的讲座。
content.en_text: You will hear part of a lecture on rural development.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间查看问题31到40。
content.en_text: First you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 大家早上好。
content.en_text: Good morning everyone.

content.cn_text: 大家应该还记得，上周我们学习了人们从西非内陆地区迁移的情况，
content.en_text: Now you'll remember that last week we looked at the migration of people from the interior

content.cn_text: 他们迁往各个沿海城镇，我们特别提到了加纳。
content.en_text: of West Africa to various coastal towns with special reference to Ghana.

content.cn_text: 今天我们将重点关注生活在北非、被称为柏柏尔人的群体。
content.en_text: Today we're going to focus on the people known as the Berbers who live in North Africa.

content.cn_text: 早在7世纪，柏柏尔人就居住在非洲这一地区，他们的社会
content.en_text: The Berbers inhabited this part of Africa as long ago as the 7th century and their society

content.cn_text: 以部落为基础，分散在多个国家，包括阿尔及利亚、摩洛哥、
content.en_text: was based on tribes which were scattered throughout a number of countries, Algeria, Morocco,

content.cn_text: 利比亚和马里。
content.en_text: Libya and Mali.

content.cn_text: 然而，在12世纪的某个时期，该地区遭到贝都因阿拉伯人的入侵。
content.en_text: However, at some point in the 12th century the area was invaded by Bedouin Arabs.

content.cn_text: 这些人摧毁了柏柏尔人的农民经济。
content.en_text: These people destroyed the Berbers' peasant economy.

content.cn_text: 因此，许多柏柏尔人离开定居点，开始过游牧生活，带着
content.en_text: As a result many Berbers left their settlements to lead a nomadic life by wandering with

content.cn_text: 他们的动物穿越沙漠和不同的山脉。
content.en_text: their animals through the deserts and across the different mountains.

content.cn_text: 几个世纪以来，许多柏柏尔人迁移到西班牙和法国等其他国家，
content.en_text: Over the centuries many Berbers migrated to other countries like Spain and France to

content.cn_text: 在那里做劳工，并带去了他们的文化和传统，他们的后代
content.en_text: work as labourers, taking with them their culture and traditions and their descendants

content.cn_text: 至今仍留在那里。
content.en_text: remained there until this day.

content.cn_text: 并不是所有柏柏尔人都过完全游牧的生活。
content.en_text: Not all the Berbers led a fully nomadic life.

content.cn_text: 他们分为三类。有些人成为农民，冬季在低地耕种，
content.en_text: There were three different groups, some became farmers cultivating the lowlands in the winter

content.cn_text: 夏季则在山上放牧他们的牲畜。
content.en_text: and grazing their flocks of animals in the mountains during the summer.

content.cn_text: 他们被称为季节性游牧民。
content.en_text: They are called seasonal nomads.

content.cn_text: 一些过完全游牧生活的柏柏尔人往往从一个绿洲迁往另一个绿洲。
content.en_text: Some Berbers who led a completely nomadic life tended to move from one oasis to another.

content.cn_text: 第三类人在绿洲旁定居，种植水果和蔬菜，比如椰枣和茄子，
content.en_text: A third group settled by the oasis and grew fruit and vegetables like dates and eggplants

content.cn_text: 还制作橄榄油用于烹饪。
content.en_text: as well as making olive oil which they used for cooking.

content.cn_text: 传统上，柏柏尔人饲养牛、绵羊和山羊，也养公牛、骡子和马。
content.en_text: Traditionally Berbers kept cattle, sheep and goats together with oxen, mules and horses.

content.cn_text: 在北非的萨赫勒地区，也就是撒哈拉沙漠以南的地区，
content.en_text: Now in the Sahel region of North Africa, that's the area south of the Sahara desert.

content.cn_text: 这个地区过去变得、现在仍在变得越来越干旱。
content.en_text: The region became and still is becoming increasingly dry and arid.

content.cn_text: 因此，柏柏尔人越来越依赖骆驼来运送家人和物品。
content.en_text: So the Berbers relied more and more on camels for transporting their families and their goods.

content.cn_text: 这些不同群体的人是如何在如此恶劣的环境中生存的呢？
content.en_text: How did these different groups of people survive in such harsh conditions?

content.cn_text: 他们住在哪里呢？
content.en_text: Where did they live?

content.cn_text: 嗯，定居在一个地方的柏柏尔人建造单层石屋来保护自己，
content.en_text: Well, Berbers who stayed put in one place built single-story stone houses for protection

content.cn_text: 这些石头来自当地采石。
content.en_text: by quarrying the local rock.

content.cn_text: 而游牧柏柏尔人则随身带着他们的住所，搭建临时帐篷。
content.en_text: Whereas nomadic Berbers carried their homes with them and erected temporary tents,

content.cn_text: 定居的柏柏尔人发展了各种小规模产业，比如制陶和编织，
content.en_text: Settled Berbers developed various small-scale industries such as pottery making and weaving,

content.cn_text: 但这些工作通常由柏柏尔妇女承担。
content.en_text: but these tasks were generally left to the Berber women.

content.cn_text: 不过，正如你可能预料的，定居社区的生活由男性管理，
content.en_text: As you might expect though, the life of a settled community was governed by the men

content.cn_text: 他们定期在村庄广场开会，讨论事务并作出决定。
content.en_text: who met regularly in the village square to discuss affairs and make decisions.

content.cn_text: 现在我们来看图阿雷格人，他们属于游牧柏柏尔群体，主要在撒哈拉沙漠中部
content.en_text: Now let's turn to the Tuaregs who belong to a nomadic Berber group and move mainly in the central

content.cn_text: 和西部、尼日尔河以北地区迁徙。
content.en_text: and western Sahara desert north of the River Niger.

content.cn_text: Tuareg 这个词来自阿拉伯语 Tawarek，意思是“被神遗弃的”。
content.en_text: The word Tuareg comes from the Arabic Tawarek and means God-forsaken.

content.cn_text: 沙漠中的图阿雷格人携带用一条条山羊皮缝合而成的帐篷。
content.en_text: Desert Tuaregs carried tents made of strips of goat skins sewn together.

content.cn_text: 制作一个完整帐篷最多需要40张皮。
content.en_text: As many as 40 skins were needed to make a complete tent.

content.cn_text: 如果没有皮，他们就编织用草或棕榈叶制成的席子，
content.en_text: If the skins weren't available, they wove mats made of grass or palm leaves

content.cn_text: 然后把它们挂在框架上，使帐篷看起来像一个隆起的圆顶。
content.en_text: and hung them over a frame so that the tent looked like a humped dome.

content.cn_text: 传统上，图阿雷格社会非常封建，并按严格等级组织，
content.en_text: Tuareg society was traditionally very feudal and organized as a strict hierarchy ranging

content.cn_text: 从贵族或上层阶级一直到劳工，而这些劳工的祖先曾经是奴隶。
content.en_text: from nobles or aristocrats downwards to laborers whose ancestors had once been slaves.

content.cn_text: 图阿雷格人以好战品质和强烈独立性著称。
content.en_text: Tuaregs were famous for their warlike qualities and fierce independence.

content.cn_text: 事实上，对图阿雷格人最大的侮辱之一，是说他的父亲死在床上，
content.en_text: In fact, one of the greatest insults was to suggest to a Tuareg that his father had died in his bed

content.cn_text: 而不是战死。图阿雷格男性有时被称为“蓝色男人”，
content.en_text: and not while fighting. Tuareg men were sometimes called blue men,

content.cn_text: 因为所有成年男性在女性、
content.en_text: as all adult males wore a dark blue veil in the presence of women,

content.cn_text: 陌生人和姻亲面前都会戴深蓝色面纱。传说图阿雷格男性如果不戴这种面纱，
content.en_text: strangers and in-laws. Legend had it that a Tuareg man couldn't be recognized

content.cn_text: 就无法被认出来。但这种戴面纱的习俗开始消失，
content.en_text: unless he was wearing this veil. But this custom of the veil began to disappear

content.cn_text: 因为越来越多图阿雷格人开始城市化并搬到城镇。
content.en_text: as more and more Tuaregs became urbanized and moved to the towns.

content.cn_text: 我之前提到过萨赫勒地区已经变得非常干旱，而且过去30年里
content.en_text: I mentioned earlier how dry the Sahel region has become and there have been very severe droughts

content.cn_text: 出现过非常严重的旱灾。这意味着生活在该地区的图阿雷格人数量已经减少。
content.en_text: over the past 30 years. This has meant that the number of Tuaregs living in the area has declined.

content.cn_text: 那些动物幸运存活下来的人已经离开撒哈拉南部，
content.en_text: Those whose animals were fortunate enough to survive have moved away from the southern Sahara

content.cn_text: 进入布基纳法索，为他们的畜群寻找新的牧场。
content.en_text: into Burkina Faso in order to find new grazing lands for their herds.

content.cn_text: 如果说有一座城市会让人联想到撒哈拉沙漠，那很可能就是廷巴克图。
content.en_text: If there's one city which people associate with the Sahara desert, it's probably Timbuktu.

content.cn_text: 但大多数人不知道的是，廷巴克图是由图阿雷格游牧民建立的，
content.en_text: But what most people don't know is that Timbuktu was founded by Tuareg nomads

content.cn_text: 时间大约在一千年前。这座城市成为跨撒哈拉商队路线的中心。
content.en_text: almost a thousand years ago. The city became the focal point of the Trans-Saharan caravan routes.

content.cn_text: 北非商人蜂拥而至做生意，黄金和盐的贸易十分繁荣。
content.en_text: North African merchants flocked there to do business and trading in gold and salt flourished.

content.cn_text: 多年来，廷巴克图被认为难以到达。但如今，这座城市的遗迹吸引了
content.en_text: For many years Timbuktu was considered inaccessible. But today what is left of the city attracts

content.cn_text: 少量游客。马里政府旅游部门雇用了一些图阿雷格人，
content.en_text: a small number of tourists. The tourism department of the Mali government employs some Tuaregs

content.cn_text: 让他们担任导游。这其实很必要，因为只有图阿雷格人
content.en_text: who act as guides. Essential really when you realize that it's only the Tuaregs

content.cn_text: 能够利用沙丘作为地标，在沙漠中辨认方向。
content.en_text: who can find their way around the desert using the sand dunes as landmarks.

content.cn_text: 好了，在我继续讨论图阿雷格人所说的塔马舍克语之前，现在我先回答问题，
content.en_text: Right I'll take questions now before I go on to discuss Tamashek, the language spoken by the Tuaregs

content.cn_text: 这种语言也有一种字母系统，叫作
content.en_text: which also has an alphabet called

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31–40

items.title: began to lead a nomadic life, moving across 31. ____________ and mountains
items.analysis: 答案解析：第31题问柏柏尔人迁徙时穿越什么和山脉。原文说 through the deserts and across the different mountains，所以答案是 Deserts。

items.title: Some Berbers, known as 32. ____________ nomads, moved twice a year.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第32题问每年迁移两次的柏柏尔人被称为什么游牧民。原文说 They are called seasonal nomads，所以答案是 seasonal。

items.title: Some Berbers settled by oases, producing fruit, vegetables, and 33. ____________ to cook with.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第33题问定居绿洲的柏柏尔人除了水果和蔬菜，还生产什么用于烹饪。原文说 making olive oil which they used for cooking，所以答案是 olive oil。

items.title: People and belongings were carried by 34. ____________.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第34题问人和物品由什么运送。原文说 relied more and more on camels for transporting their families and goods，所以答案是 camels。

items.title: Settled communities organised themselves by holding regular meetings in the 35. ____________ of the village.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第35题问定居社区在哪里举行定期会议。原文说 met regularly in the village square，所以答案是 square。

items.title: If goatskins were unavailable, tents were made from 36. ____________ or palm-leaf mats.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第36题问如果没有山羊皮，帐篷会用什么或棕榈叶席子制成。原文说 mats made of grass or palm leaves，所以答案是 grass。

items.title: Tuareg labourers were the descendants of 37. ____________.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第37题问图阿雷格劳工是谁的后代。原文说 labourers whose ancestors had once been slaves，所以答案是 slaves。

items.title: Adult men always wore a 38. ____________ veil.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第38题问成年男性总是戴什么颜色的面纱。原文说 wore a dark blue veil，所以答案是 dark blue。

items.title: Some Tuaregs moved to Burkina Faso with their 39. ____________.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第39题问一些图阿雷格人带着什么迁往布基纳法索。原文这里说 animals survived和人一起走了，所以答案是 animals。我个人感觉这里的herds也是指的animals，按道理来说是都可以的；但是因为herds也能指一群人或动物，这样对比起来的话animals稍微好一点。

items.title: Some Tuaregs now work as 40. ____________ for the Malian Department of Tourism.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第40题问现在一些图阿雷格人为马里旅游部门做什么工作。原文说 act as guides，所以答案是 guides。

groups.source_collect:
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
The Berbers
• lived in various countries in North Africa
• were driven from their homes during the 12th century
• began to lead a nomadic life, moving across $31$ and mountains
Different Berber Lifestyles
• Some Berbers, known as $32$ nomads, moved twice a year.
• Some Berbers were completely nomadic.
• Some Berbers settled by oases, producing fruit, vegetables, and $33$ to cook with.
• People and belongings were carried by $34$.
• Settled Berbers lived in houses.
• Nomadic Berbers used tents.
• Settled communities organised themselves by holding regular meetings in the $35$ of the village.
The Tuaregs
• If goatskins were unavailable, tents were made from $36$ or palm-leaf mats.
• Tuareg labourers were the descendants of $37$.
• Adult men always wore a $38$ veil.
• Some Tuaregs moved to Burkina Faso with their $39$.
Tuaregs and Timbuktu
• Timbuktu was settled by Tuaregs around 1000 years ago.
• The city built wealth on the salt and gold trades.
• Some Tuaregs now work as $40$ for the Malian Department of Tourism.

source_credit: 此内容由🍠@Listening 永不虾滑整理，仅作为分享供个人使用；禁止商用、倒卖和引流。有问题请先仔细看🍠这个账号的置顶和分享贴。没有团队，不是机构，纯个人分享。督学和其他相关课程请咨询主页另一个号@心情有点虾滑（不回任何机经相关消息）。
